title: Inside the mutually beneficial relationship of shipping magnate Saadé and President Macron
subhed: 'The shipping giant at the heart of French power' (1/4) The French leader and the head of the company CMA CGM have developed a relationship that is as respectful as it is useful for both parties, given how closely the interests of the family-owned group and the French state are intertwined.

Sandra Laugier, philosopher: 'In France as in the US, never has freedom of expression been so invoked by its enemies'
subhed: The recent crisis that erupted at Grasset is symptomatic of a new wave of brutality in the public sphere, argues academic Sandra Laugier for Le Monde. According to Laugier, popular culture is far more resistant than certain intellectual circles, which wrongly believe themselves to be protected from the far right's predations.

title: Suspicious transactions on oil markets: 'On Wall Street, this sense of impunity is clearly widespread'
subhed: Insider trading is now commonplace on the New York Stock Exchange, where profits linked to oil sales connected to the situation in Iran are estimated to have reached several hundred million dollars, reports Le Monde's New York correspondent Nicolas Chapuis.

title: Gaming clubs officially set up shop in Paris
subhed: In early 2026, seven gaming establishments, where slot machines are prohibited, were made permanent after an eight-year trial period. The massive Partouche Casino Club, which is not a traditional casino, will have its grand opening on Tuesday.

title: In Los Angeles, the World Cup still struggles to find its place in the sun
subhed: One month before the kickoff of the 2026 World Cup, Le Monde correspondents share a glimpse of their country's relationship with football.

Paris's La Villette reinvents itself as an urban climate laboratory
subhed: Forty years after its inauguration by former French president François Mitterrand, the green lung of northeast Paris is transforming into a vibrant haven where animals, plants and children coexist.

title: In Spain, the woman priest challenging the Vatican
subhed: Christina Moreira advocates for women's right to the priesthood, and was excommunicated by the Vatican for seeking the role. She continues her vocation as a parish priest in Galicia, disturbing figures high up in the Catholic Church.

title: Who is Iyad Ag Ghali, the jihadist behind the destabilization of Mali?
subhed: A central figure in Sahelian jihad for nearly two decades, pursued in turn by the French and the Russians, Ghali led the offensive against the junta in Bamako. Since 2012, the jihadist and leader of Jama'at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin (JNIM) has softened his rhetoric, seeking to appear more approachable and less radical.
